title: mentha arvensis var. procumbens
intro: Mentha arvensis var. procumbens, also known as field mint or American wild mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in reducing inflammation and acting as an antiseptic, due to its high concentration of menthol and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of field mint include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, soothe respiratory problems, and provide relief from headaches and fever. Field mint can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or extracting the essential oil through steam distillation.
